all_clusters.headline: Treasury nullifies Agriculture Ministry’s grain import levy – calls it legally invalid
all_clusters.description:
THE Ministry of Finance has declared as invalid a grain import levy imposed on millers by the Agriculture Ministry through Statutory Instrument (SI) 87 of 2025 and urged authorities to immediately repeal it. A letter dated March 3, from the Ministry of Finance’s permanent secretary George Guvamatanga to Chief Secretary to the President and Cabinet […]

all_clusters.headline: Zimbabwe Extends Fuel Price Cushion Measures Amid Middle East Supply Pressures
all_clusters.description:
Zimbabwe Extends Fuel Price Cushion Measures Amid Middle East Supply Pressures Government will maintain measures introduced to shield motorists and businesses from rising fuel costs as authorities continue to monitor the impact of instability in the Middle East on global energy markets. Finance and Economic Development Minister Mthuli Ncube said the interventions, first rolled out […]

all_clusters.headline: Namib Minerals Advances Redwing Mine Restart as Dewatering Programme Remains on Schedule
all_clusters.description:
HARARE – Namib Minerals has reported steady progress in the restart of its Redwing Gold Mine in Zimbabwe, with dewatering operations advancing according to plan and laying the groundwork for the mine’s return to production. The company announced that since the commencement of dewatering activities on 29 January 2026, approximately 544,570 cubic metres of water […]

all_clusters.headline: Zimbabwe’s sugar tax funds US$27 million radiotherapy machines, to slash cancer treatment backlog
all_clusters.description:
Zimbabwe’s controversial sugar tax is beginning to deliver tangible results, with new radiotherapy machines worth US$27 million being installed at Parirenyatwa Group of Hospitals as government moves to tackle a treatment backlog affecting hundreds of patients. Finance Minister Professor Mthuli Ncube on Tuesday toured Parirenyatwa Hospital and expressed satisfaction with progress on the installation of […]

all_clusters.headline: China’s factory activity slows in May, raising questions over its economy
all_clusters.description:
BEIJING — China’s factory activity was flat in May, according to an official survey released Sunday, raising questions about how much further the country’s economy can shield itself from the fallout of the ongoing Iran war and pressure on demand. The official manufacturing purchasing managers index moderated to 50 from 50.3 in April, according to […]

all_clusters.headline: Millennium Heights powers ahead with solar innovation
all_clusters.description:
WestProp Holdings, developer of Millennium Heights, has redefined sustainable urban living with the installation of a onemegawatt solar plant that now covers its 54-bay car park. More parking bays on upcoming apartment blocks will be fitted with solar covering, generating more energy for the estate. As part of its promise when pioneering the 1,000 unit […]

all_clusters.headline: Global fuel shocks bite Zimbabwe consumers
How UAE became Zimbabwe’s top customer, buying half of everything it exports
all_clusters.description:
Business Insider Africa The United Arab Emirates has overtaken South Africa as Zimbabwe’s largest export destination, marking a significant shift in the country’s trade landscape. UAE-bound exports accounted for 50% of Zimbabwe’s total export earnings in the first quarter of 2026, according to official data. Bilateral trade exceeded $5 billion in 2025 and is projected […]
